title: Three Recommendations for Solo RPG Systems / Combos (+1 Bonus Recommendation)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZtUTR0E7VPY

Daniel (@dungeondive) gives his three concrete starting-point recommendations for solo RPGs — each capped at roughly $30 to keep the barrier low — plus one bonus pick for total newcomers.

**Bonus (gamebook newcomers):** *Fabled Lands.* A multi-volume keyword-tracking gamebook series with light RPG bookkeeping; the open-world structure trains you for sandbox play without demanding a system yet.

**Pick 1 (weirdo metal):** *Mörk Borg* with the *Solitary Defilement* solo system and the *Feretory* supplement. Tiny core rules, vast supplement ecosystem, and a death-metal aesthetic you can dial down to standard dark fantasy if it's not your thing.

**Pick 2 (traditional dungeon crawl):** *D100 Dungeon* core book plus the *World Builder*. Roll-and-write OSR comfort food with a hex-crawl layer for adventures between dungeons — Daniel calls *World Builder* one of his favourite expansions for any game, despite the layout being rough.

**Pick 3 (the broken-record answer):** *Scarlet Heroes* by Kevin Crawford. A single book containing the full Target-20 system, solo rules, urban/wilderness/dungeon procedures, and his Adventure Tag generator. Pair with the $5 *White Box* if the pan-Asian bestiary doesn't suit you. Daniel's all-in-one pick for sword-and-sorcery solo play.

----

Of these three — Mörk Borg, D100 Dungeon, Scarlet Heroes — which served as your actual on-ramp into solo, and did it still hold up after you'd moved on?
